Monitoring Station No. 1 is halfway up the volcano. We still have a long walk ahead of us.
I'm fine. My energy can keep up still.
Hmm...
The scholar lowers her head. her heart is rending with a sharp, piercing pain.
Just who is the terrified one?
The volcano is right in front of her, but she cannot bring herself to take a single step forward.
Professor Keller. Are you okay?
Would you like to take my hand?
Streams colored dark red can be seen slowly climbing their way through the mountain rocks like the blood veins in one's body, all while the enormous thing breathes strenuously.
From the earth's core to this cliff, they flowed through the ages, enduring an intense heat and utter silence.
This path seems to not be nearly as long as Adele had imagined. With the strength she has, she can still climb onto this mountain.
But this path also seems to be much longer than she had imagined. It's long enough for her memories to be rinsed countless of times at the bottom of her heart.
This is where we set up the monitoring station.
This equipment... Has someone been here?
But there are no other footprints on the mountain...
The Originium energy response hasn't changed, but the temperature measurement instruments logged a high response...
She takes a look around and can make out only the dark mountain gravel and molten rocks in the shadows.
How strange...
The event has most likely ended. Let's tune the instruments and head back.
Kahn, what are you doing here?
(Out of breath)
I heard... you two... climbed the volcano...
Why...?
Adele was worried because the monitoring station's indices were fluctuating, so we came here to inspect.
It was probably caused by accident. We're preparing to evacuate to a safe observation point.
The volcanic activity is definitely starting to pick up. The eruption might happen sooner than expected...
Yeah, we need to go.
Stay right there, Keller.
Kahn?
The man, still out of breath, is trembling. He pulls out a file from his chest, and the moment Keller sees its cover, her expression changes immediately.
September 1095, on Mount Unna, why did you suddenly leave the survey team...?
And in November that year, you didn't attend the Naumanns' funeral, but someone said they saw you at a symposium with the military at Wilhelm University.
On top of that, not even Adele has seen Katia Naumann's research data. What is it doing in a letter between you and the Leithanian military?!
Keller, answer me!
Professor Keller...?
Adele, I told you that I'd share everything with you when this observation is done...
Kahn, it's dangerous here. We should—
Keller... Look where we're standing. The Naumanns were here, on this very volcano.
Are you still going to lie, even here?!
Hmm... I'm not really one to interfere with human affairs.
But this isn't the place for a row...
Oof... This is just the right temperature. Hot springs are fun, but the temperature's a tad bit too peaceful.
This temperature keeps my head clear. Helps me think about the real important questions.
Say... Which did the planet birth first? Lava or rocks?

Let's start from the very beginning. 'Lava' wasn't 'lava,' and 'rock' wasn't 'rock.' All thing are a cycle, and they take different shapes during their changes, and so they were given different names.

Man is always trying to use assertive language to delimit the indescribable natures of the world, almost like it's the only way they can get a good night's sleep. This, too, goes to show their lack of wisdom...
Hey, watch out there. Don't splash all that lava everywhere. It's dangerous. It's not that likely, but what if someone just happens to walk by—
Hm? What's with the ruckus... Is somebody trying to join us?

The enormous monster beneath their feet is breathing heavier and heavier by the second. The temperature in their surroundings has risen quite a bit. The lava spewed out from the volcano's crater rapidly cools and turns into rocks that come rolling down.
Kahn, Professor Keller... This place is dangerous... We should leave...
Adele, you get out of here first. This is something that I need to settle with her, right here and now!
Kahn, don't be absurd!
When you left them there on Mount Unna, what they had to face was much worse!
Do you even have a conscience? Is admitting to what you've done harder than facing death?!
Kahn... We need to hurry...
I won't be able to block the next wave...
Watch out! Behind you!
Adele lifts her staff, but the rocks are falling like rain. Her Originium Arts are unable to melt all of them down.
She focuses on protecting her allies, yet she can't hear their warnings or the sound of the danger behind her.
I saw you the past few days, but it looks like nobody else can.
I don't know where you came from, but you're the only one who's willing to hang out with me. I know, you like strawberry ice cream, too, right?
(Lies down obediently)
Look, our home used to be right under that volcano there, but it's going to go away soon.
I actually know... I was abandoned there, and Harley and Enis found me.
I don't know why I was abandoned, but if my real parents ever come looking for me, will they still be able to find me?
*sigh*... Daddy is gone, and Enis is leaving. Little lamb, where do you think our home really is? Where is the friend who ate strawberry ice cream with me a long time ago?
(Unsatisfied groan)
Hey—
The ocean doesn't answer her. It merely sits between the two Siestas as always.
Suddenly, the girl notices that the calm, quiet shoreline has gotten closer to her at some point. The seawater gradually drowned the dry surface and has reached her feet.
Hmm...?
Liv!
Their surroundings suddenly fall silent.
The bubbling lava, the falling rocks. All of it are suddenly without sound whatsoever, as though time itself has ground to a halt.

Adele isn't sure if it is her hearing aids that have failed or that she has once again fallen into a dream. All she can feel is that she is enveloped by a formless warmth, as though she has fallen into a cozy mattress.
Poor little one, you seem to be frightened. *sigh*, why did you climb the volcano at a time like this?
Mr. Dolly? So this is where you've been!
I thought you'd left... Where are Keller and Kahn?! Did the lambs cause the volcanic anomaly? I... Am I already dead...?
Hey, hey! Calm down, little one. I know you have a lot of questions, but slow down. We'll take it one step at a time.
You are alive and well, and so are your buddies, but I won't let them see me. Once they wake up from this, all they'll think is that they had a nice little nap.
As for all that strange equipment on the volcano... Terribly sorry. I told them to be extra careful—I'm not lying this time!
But I wasn't able to keep them in check. They were being so rowdy with their fun... And I'm not lying about this, either.
Having fun... on a volcano?
Of course! Didn't I tell you? This is second best thing on the planet! Volcano surfing!

Adele lifts her head up and looks into the distance. There is lava gushing out of the crater sporadically, but she can faintly make out silhouettes of critters enjoying themselves. At the same time, there are many more creatures happily making their way toward the crater.
The mountain is covered in a blanket of white wool, almost like clouds have come falling down from the sky and draped themselves over it.
Mr. Dolly, these sheep aren't just your clones, are they...?
Ohh? You've realized it, have you?
Just as I said, I've met a lot of interesting folk during the long, long time I've been around.
Oh, don't get the wrong idea. They aren't 'souls' or anything.
There's no way to keep lives that have been lost. I simply copied how they looked and made all these little ones... It's like... a personal collection? I thought you'd like them.
That sounds... kind of cruel...
Look at me talking on and on. I only just noticed that you've found the fur too. Yeah... it's been way too long since the last time I saw them.
Just then, another scar appears on her old, tattered protective gear.
I should've guessed.
It's an apt metaphor, isn't it? Animals need very thick fur to protect themselves—same for humans.
When I see you standing here like that, it's like I'm looking at those two old friends of mine. I really miss them.
Oh, you earn no praise from me for inheriting their exquisite mantle. It's just I'm guessing you miss them too. Poor girl, why can't you just open up and admit it?
No need to act tough. You shouldn't keep yourself so on edge, either. No one will deny a girl your age the right to feel sad or afraid. You need to learn to not act tough.
If you're tired, stop and take a break or cry your heart out.
Mr. Dolly, is this scavenger hunt just a game?
It's true that it's not really all that mysterious of a puzzle. I just wanted you to have some fun, Adele.
I had lots of fun during my time in Siesta.
I don't understand. Why are you taking such good care of me in particular...?
It's simple. A girl as lovely as you is a rare sight on this world.
Misfortune may find you often... but you have no less love than anybody else.
Actually, I saw you a long time ago, back in Leithanien.
A little girl always earnestly reading her books in that tiny, little spire.
As the child of two genius academics, many had high expectations of her. She was seen as a gifted Caster and scholar.

Outstanding, remarkably talented individuals like her are always burdened with achieving results to match their talent. Even if they are inflicted with an incurable disease, they are seen by others as a tenacious role model.
Young Adele, has anyone ever asked you what you wanted?

Take a look. There are so many people and so many lives all around the world. You know volcanoes like the back of your hoof, but there are so many fun things out there besides volcanoes. Why not see for yourself?
Thanks... Mr. Dolly. I've... had a lot of fun these past few days...
I can smell half a lie. Just half, but we'll leave it like this for now.
It's nearly time. I really wanted to invite you to come try lava surfing with me, but the human body is too weak.
I'll take you three somewhere safe. Then, I'll have to be ready for the opening of Volcano Paradise.
So long, Adele.
Thanks, Mr. Dolly... Will we meet again?
Perhaps... No, maybe I should say we will meet again for sure.
I hope that the next time we meet, I will get to see a truly happy Adele.
Promise me this?
Yeah!
The fluffy creature shakes its body a little, walks forward a few steps, and lightly brushes the girl's cheek with its snout.
Oh! I almost forgot. I owe you a reward for finding all three things.
Mr. Dolly, do you know anything else about... the death of my parents?
Hrmm...
What I mean is... The thing you've been looking for... isn't 'Volcano Watchers?'
You've been talking about Volcano Watchers?!
S-Surprise...?
I thought...
There used to be a lot of Volcano Watchers growing on Mount Siesta's hills. They look pretty, and they are tasty. That's why the little ones ate most of them up.
Then I realized if they kept on eating them, it wouldn't be long before they went extinct. That's why I planted some of them in a secret spot behind their backs.
So the Volcano Watchers aren't extinct yet... That's good news...
Then where did you plant the flowers?
Oh, just on top of a hill around the back of the volcano.
By volcano... You mean this one, the one that's about to erupt...?
Er... yeah.
Enis?
Are you... leaving...?
(Out of breath)
Why did you... run out here all by yourself...?
I thought... you weren't ever going to come back.
Are you kidding me...?
When you're on the adventure team... you never work alone.
Let's go home.
The seawater has risen above their soles, making it difficult to even walk.
Hurry!
At first, the waves were just a hazy line at the far reach of their vision, but in just the blink of an eye, they are right here in front of them.
Enis tightly clutches his sister as the waves beat against his less than robust back, causing him to stagger.
Another, taller wave is approaching them.
(There's no way...)
(Let's try this—)
Enis tries his hardest to feel the 'rock' in his body. It's close to his heart. Every time his heart beats, he can feel its hard texture.
A rock the size of a man rises from the ground in front of the siblings and immediately crumbles just barely withstanding the crashing of the wave.
There is an intense pain in his chest.
Enis!
Look... Didn't I tell you...? I know magic...
Enis wipes the blood on his face and pulls his sister so she isn't hurt by the crumbling gravel.
Quick, hide behind me!
One, two...
Seemingly formed by the white waves, a countless number of fluffy creatures rush toward them from the coast. They stack together and form a wall, or more precisely, a dam.
The waves clash against the furry dam, completely absorbed by it the very next second.
I must've gone crazy...
What exactly is this...?
Having absorbed the seawater, their white wool has grown even puffier, enough to keep the siblings safe from the dangerous waves.
After who knows how long, the ground has finally stopped shaking, and the waves gradually start to calm.
Are... Are we saved?
(Shivers) (Shakes to dry body)
Th-Thank you...?
(Happy bleat)
Just like cotton candy dissolving in the water, the beastling suddenly disappears.
I guess that's... a near-death hallucination...?
Enis!
The night deepens, and at some point in time, the bustling crowd's clamor gradually started to fade.
Someone has taken out a guitar and started playing a gentle melody.
Almost like some kind of tacit understanding, everyone looks in the same direction.
A pillar of smoke gradually rises from the volcano's caldera, followed by spews of dark red and ash gray. The ground is trembling slightly underneath their feet.
We're so far out. We definitely won't get caught in that, right...?
*sigh*... It still came.
Oh... It really erupted...
The people in the crowd hold the hands of those around them. Parents hug their children tight, as pedestrians begin to hold their breaths.

As though they all finally feel a weight off their shoulders, or perhaps like a giant boulder has finally crushed their hearts, sighs and quiet mumblings can be heard amongst the crowd, yet this just makes the whole street seem even more quiet.
This continues for a very, very long time, until the crowd snaps back from the sight before them.

I can still recall that back when I was a young boy... my parents would take me to this little hill. It was full of flowers and trees whose names I never knew. There were so many of them, and they smelled so wonderful every spring.

My father was allergic to their pollen, so he would wait for us at the bottom of the hill. My mother and I would bend the flowers' branches and weave garlands. We ran and ran across the hill, and waved down to the bottom of the hill...
...That won't ever happen again...
Mr. Tom...
It's nothing, girl. I'm just thinking about the past... I used to sell ice cream every day, meeting all kinds of children.
Now my children are all in Columbia. They didn't come back home, even for a time like this.
Sir, your children are far away. It's possible that they didn't know Siesta's volcano was going to erupt today. The might be on their way, or they could have a package still in transit...
I came here because I wanted to build a logistics center that connected all regions and shortened the distances between them.
Maybe someday, you'll get things from different places more quickly, and your children will know how you're doing that much faster.
But we're still a long way away from achieving that... Just now, I was thinking that I might be able to do something to help...
What's that...?
If anyone needs to send a postcard to family abroad, Mountaincomm Trade could deliver them for free the next few days.
By the time they get them, or when they come back after they get their postcards, Siesta will be a better place than it is now.
I'll do my best to make this a better place, too. It's a small wish of mine, aside from my commercial ambitions.
Thanks, kid.
It's such a nice thing to be able to write your family...
When I was studying in Columbia, I would sometimes get homesick at night. I always wanted to write home, but I couldn't afford international postage, so all I ever did was cry myself to sleep...

That's when I'd remember a poem that means something like, wheresoever someone is, the twin moons in the night sky are always the same, and if we look at the moons, even the greatest distances are nothing.
Does Siesta have any songs like that...?
The old man clears his throat. His beard slightly twitches, as though he remembers something, and starts singing a song stiltedly.
The volcanic ash slowly spreads, covering half the sky.
Each day I linger, aside my dreams ♪
I've lost my way, I'm a fowl without wings ♪
I grasp at hope, I'm holdin' on out ♪
Oh on the road, will I come tumblin' down ♪
And maybe somethin' scares in me, if tomorrow's sun I see ♪
But I'm just headin' far, I'm no wanderer ♪
...I'm just headin' far, I'm no wanderer ♪
'Cause right out there... I've been lookin' to be ♪
Lady Ceylon.
Schwarz! What are you doing here?!
I had a few missions for Rhodes Island, but I came as soon as I was finished with them.
It took me forever to find you. I'd been searching the crowd for the better part of the day. I have your favorite tea in my bag. I'll give it to you as soon as we get back.
And... I also wanted to come back...
They see the rocks in their memories crumble, as a magnificent evening glow they had never before seen rises from the top of the volcano.

A pink smoke covers half of the sky and gradually flutters downward toward the ground. Although they did not see it for themselves, they know a memory that belonged only to them has now been forever buried underneath the white volcanic ash.
Am I seeing things... How did the top of the volcano turn white?
What are you talking about? I don't see it...
Siesta... has been wiped out.
I think... 'reborn' might be the more appropriate word here.
I say we remember this day forevermore... How about we call it the 'Volcano Festival?'

lines.st: Sweet peaceful dreams to you each evening ♪
Not a worry, not a sweat ♪
For what's gone is gone, and the future will come ♪
The sun will rise tomorrow, happy days ♪

lines.x: See you, Siesta.
See you... Siesta
...Don't be sad, Lady Ceylon. Tomorrow is a new day.
Right... tomorrow is a new day.
Hey! You ugly-ass sheep...
Keep my vinyls out your stinkin' mouth...! No chewin' my limited edition shirt, either!
Watch that lava! Dammit! Heat degrades a vinyl's sound!
You think this gonna wear out my patience? You got another thing comin'. I'd do this ten thousand years...
Dolly! I swear, I'ma feed your clones to the wolves...!
Some ecologists have categorized all the fowlbeasts on this great land into two categories—'Wayfowls' and 'Homefowls'.

The former will, owing to climatic and ecological shifts, constantly relocate as they look for the most suitable habitat for survival; while the latter once settled in their habitat, will stay for good, and stop at nothing to protect their homes.

This biological theory was later borrowed by historians to suggest that 'all conflict between the races on this continent stems from the differing views between those who can only live in one place and those who drift nomadically.'
Afterwards came yet another supplementary addition to this viewpoint.
There were originally no Homefowls on this great land, but as the fowlbeasts gradually gathered in places, built their homes together, and created a collective memory, the Homefowls came to be.
I won't miss it this time. According to the route on this map, Siesta is just right over this peak.
Siesta, is right here...
She looks all around her, but only snow fills her vision.
Where... where am I?!
Yo. Been a while.
Oh? What brings the Emperor here to Siesta, without an Obsidian Festival to draw him? And your shirt, it seems something burned a hole through it...?
I'ma ax you to change the topic here.
I collected a good amount of material in the last few days, and should be able to quickly put out an album after I return to Columbia.
Ain't a lotta musicians I look forward to, Byrd, but you one of 'em.
I can't help but recall the first Obsidian Festival I attended, when I was chatting with Barbara about the future of Siesta she saw.
If one day, when this city leaves the volcano and obsidian, leaves the charming scenery and coastline, just what will remain of it?
She said that music is a kind of carrier, but the most important carriers are actually the people living here.
As long as there are people willing to sing in joy or even sorrow, as long as they're willing to enjoy the sunset afterglow for a moment when evening comes, Siesta will remain the same.
Young people can't get all hung up on nostalgia. Makes the music stale.
That's a little too harsh, Emperor.
Even in our fleeting lifetimes, you can feel lost if you don't occasionally look back at the path you've traveled.
You have to look back from time to time, to see if you've left anything precious behind on the road.
So now that all the past's done been buried under that volcano, how's about an Obsidian Festival 2.0? Just so happens I got me some investment plans.
If we want to revive it, should we come up with a new name too?
Volcano Festival? That seems a bit too obvious... Oh, right. I did see an adorable creature on this trip. How does 'Marshmallow Festival' sound?
Dayum, Byrd. Your taste in ain't one thousandth as good as your music.
The volcano observation data is all here, Professor Keller.
We've collected some really valuable information, and I'll go back after sorting these out.
Thank you for all your work...
You really are already a scholar in your own right. Katia and Magna would be proud.
This protective gear is also... compared to the one in the display, it really is...
The scholar's voice trails off, and she stares at the familiar coat for a long, long time.
I want to scale more volcanoes in my mother's gear. It'll keep me safe. I want to thank you too for your guidance during this time, I've really learned a lot.
By the way, where's Kahn?
He's gone back to Leithanien early for work.
Professor Keller, I want to apologize to you on Kahn's behalf. He must have misunderstood something...
...Don't mention it.
Adele... have you never suspected me of anything?
Adele lightly shakes her head.
I don't know...
I know very little about that period of time, and it seems like everyone was deliberately hiding it from me.
But I saw my parents' notes, and that photo too...
I just can't believe that someone who has been them on so many volcanoes, and through so many dangers, would ever do something to betray them...
There's something I want to show you.
These papers are probably what Kahn was trying to find.
Aside from the copy stored in the Leithanian archives, the only other that exists is with me.
These are discarded documents, so it's nothing confidential, but I was only hoping no one would ever come looking for them again...
The scholar retrieves a cardboard box from the bookcase, the personal effects of two other scholars within the small container.
The aged document case has words like 'Highly Confidential' written on it, with an 'Obsolete' stamped on top.
'Projekt Regenschauer'.
Is this... Vati's military research project...?
Katia and Magna both wished to stay pure scholars their whole lives, and indeed they strived to fulfil this ideal.
But reality means it can be hard to stay pure...

After the coup in Leithanien more than two decades ago, they were always getting caught up in disputes between the nobles. So they had to make some compromises in order to continue their research work.
Katia handed over some of his research results to the army in exchange for a relatively peaceful academic environment, while I helped him with some of the follow-up liaison work.
But they pinned their hopes on you.
They hoped to keep these impure disputes out of your life, so that you could study whatever you wanted without any distractions.
That is also my wish, as well...

I hope that when people talk of the Naumanns many years later, they will only remember them as pure academics and great scientists, and that their only child can explore the depths of knowledge without any worries.
Vati and Mutti... were their deaths, really an accident...?
During that Mount Unna observation, an envoy from Leithanien paid us another visit, just as we were preparing to scale the volcano.
I was hoping they could complete their work without any inference, so I offered to help them shrug off the military. I didn't expect that mishap...
Adele, I dare not ask for your forgiveness...
Professor Keller, why do you think this is your fault?
I won't question my parents' belief in scientific research because of this, and I certainly wouldn't question you either, Professor Keller.
All of you tried your best for an ideal, so isn't it just too unfair to take on any more blame...?
I think that's how Vati and Mutti would feel too.
I will always remember the summer when Katia and Magna came to Siesta. Meeting them was the luckiest thing to ever happen to me.
That day, a pair of strange foreign tourists walked into the coffee shop, sat at the table next to mine, and changed my life.
I got to see a wider, a more beautiful world beyond what you read in the books. I had the best time of my life.
From Siesta to Leithanien, and many more places besides, many more volcanoes, and many more adventures.
I wish I could accompany them on another journey.
Professor Keller, can I give you a hug?
The scholar takes the girl into her arms, gently resting her forehead against hers.
Professor Keller, can I stay here for a while, just by myself?
Of course.
The girl flips through the old documents one after another, and finds a handwritten letter at the bottom of the pile.

lines.st: '...The above information comes from two scientists who are incomparably devoted to science.
'They spent and gave their whole lives to scientific research, in hopes of using knowledge to protect humanity, seeking a safer and better home.
'I hope this will be taken in earnest, and this research is used for the right cause.'

lines.x: Professor Keller's name... is actually...
In the corner of the letter is a beautiful signature.

lines.st: 'Adele Keller'

lines.x: Liv!
Lay back, and don’t move.
How's Liv—
Calm down, she's gone home. That little one was scared out of her wits, but suffered no real injuries—you're the one that needs worrying.
Good... that's good to hear...
The young man drops back into his bed in relief.
Just how... many days was I out for?
Three whole days.
Your mom and even your siblings came to visit you everyday. Do you know just how worried they were?
Spare me your lecture just this once, Dr. Ceylon.
I was also... saving someone, right...? I'm so ready to lecture that little sister of mine...
Hmph, still trying to hide it? Do you think a doctor wouldn't be able to tell?
You've got some nerve to actually study Arts in secret. Do you know just how high your Blood Originium-Crystal Density was, when you were wheeled in here?
Is this...?
Is this treatment... expensive?
Heh, even if you were to get a job at Rhodes Island, I estimate it'd cost you a couple of paychecks.
Haha... I guess I really have no choice now...
But speaking of which, I'm currently preparing to establish a treatment center for Infected people here in Siesta. I'm just starting up, so I'll need plenty of helping hands.
Would you like to consider staying to help me out?
Dr. Ceylon, thanks for the offer, but I already made a deal with my family... I won't be staying in Siesta for a while.
If it's possible, I'd rather take a look at the landship you mentioned that goes to many places.
Even if they aren't by my side, family will always be family. I can try to live for myself, and do what I really want to do. I also want to see the sights that my dad has seen.
Being an explorer sure sounds like a pretty cool job.
What's that sound?
Oh, it must be Swire's construction crew. She really moves fast. The ink on the contract probably hasn't even dried yet.
White Volcano...
Dr. Ceylon! I have to head back first, I'll pay my bills later!
Done with work?
Mhm, that was the last patient. Phew... I've been so busy working on the treatment center these last few days that I haven't had the time to hear about what you've been up to...
Just some missions for Rhodes Island is all. I went to a few more countries, and got to know more Operators too.
When this vacation is over, I might go on some longer missions, or be stationed somewhere for a while.
Hm, I do want to come with you, but I really can't leave right now. Besides, you wouldn't want me to, right?
Yes, it would be meaningless otherwise.
How about I take you around here for the next few days then?
Sure. Siesta has become even prettier than the last time I was here.
I'm sure you'll see an even better Siesta on your next visit.
I believe in you, Milady.

Autumn has yet to fully arrive, the sunlight is still warm, but the weather seems to suddenly cool down considerably. There seems to be more people playing in the streets, and a few surprising queues leaking out of some shops.
(Please, please don't tear it down, not yet!)
(At the very least, let me have one last look...)
Enis? Aren't you supposed to be in a hospital bed—
Did they already tear down the White—
Enis spots the brand new signage at the shop entrance before he finishes speaking.
In addition to replacing the damaged light bulb, Harley has also added a little doodle herself—three marshmallowy sheep, one big and two small.
Who said renovation meant a tear down? They're going to build a new water park here.
A water park?! Then, what about the logistics center?
I heard that city hall redid their plans, and since Siesta still needs to retain some of our tourist attractions, this street is one of them.

Pelipper wrapped up and sold several of his shops to a foreign group, saying an investor felt that the previous events had a pretty good reception, so there's money to be made in building a waterpark here.
So our little shop might be able to stay open for a while longer.
We went all through that, and in the end...
Mom, whose car is that...?
Oh, someone who came to the shop a few days ago, that rich girl? She parked it here.
A brand new pickup truck in pure white is parked in front of the shop, with a small note pressed under the wiper.

lines.st: 'Helping a friend with some minor troubles.'

lines.x: That lady sure loves to act cool...
...Thanks a bunch.
'Hello, Mr. Adams Schwire. As always, I first wish you good health.'
'......then I've won this time.'
*feeble gasp*
Do you want me to switch off the television, or call your doctor?
My unseemly granddaughter.
She's finally done a...
Huff...
How many years has it been, seeing a face like that on you? I should have called Lin over too.
Don't get cocky... Sooner or later, you too will...
Beware the youth... Wei Yenwu.
Time... is on their side.
They will always, one day, stand up to us.
I can't wait... to see... when Hui-chieh... sends one... to you...
Neither can I.
Ha... haha...
Hahahaha...
Hahahahaha!
Ahahaagh—
Hey, sick old tiger? Lo bing fu?
Hey. HEY!
Get the doctor!
Dear Mutti and Vati:
I hope the both of you are doing well.
I'm doing fine myself, I came to Siesta, and had an unforgettable summer.
It's very different from the ones you two have seen, but it's still a very interesting city.
I climbed up Mount Siesta too. I successfully completed the observation, and obtained some really valuable research data.
I met one of your old friends here. No, I should say I met two of them.

They told me many stories, about the coffee shop encounter, that one time with the sulfuric acid pool, the stories that happened in the laboratories, and all kinds of things I never knew about you both, things that happened when both of you were younger.
And the other 'person' told me that 'The past isn't truly over. They're always by your side, in one way or another.'
I have even more reason to believe it now, after going through a magical experience.
Maybe some parts were made-up, but I'm willing to, and hope to believe in them, just like the bedtime stories I used to hear.
Vati and Mutti, I have to admit that I've never been as strong as I thought.

I was afraid I wouldn't be able to hear anything ever again, that the sights in front of me would turn blurry, and it wouldn't be volcanoes or research that eventually took my life, but the pain and suffering of Oripathy.
If I can, I hope to visit all the volcanoes you two visited, and answer every question posed in your research notes.
I want to take a chance, and try going on a boat ride in a sulfuric acid lake, pretending to be a tiny stone burning in the lava.
Fear is not the opposite of courage, just as death is not the opposite of life. I'm passionate about researching volcanoes, just as I am with my life.
Or maybe I should come up with a much merrier way of going about this. But is it too late for me to understand all of this now?
Actually, I have no idea where to send this letter, I just wanted to let the two of you know...
I'm doing fine here.
Want to mail something, Miss Adele? Leave it to me, and I can send it when I'm delivering the mail.
Where is this headed?
Um...
Actually, never mind. I think I'd like to mail it myself.
Mr. Enis, this flower is—
Ah. I dug it out from the hillside when I was looking for obsidian back then. I wanted to give it to Liv as a present, but the petal colors seem to have changed since I came down from the volcano.
Oh! Did you get it on Mount Siesta?
Yeah, at the back of the volcano.
By the way, the thing you originally came to Siesta for? I found that too.
(What a beautiful volcano.)
(A volcano is most beautiful when it erupts, even if it only lasts for a short moment.)
(How long will we have to wait for its next eruption?)
(That doesn't matter, we can stay here for a while longer.)
(We have plenty of time.)
